teaching_arc.entry_point: Students know Riemann sums from Topic 23; motivate net change under a varying rate curve.
teaching_arc.progression: Rate hook → definition as limit → Riemann-sum warm-up → visual_lab convergence → net vs signed area → properties table → geometric rectangle example → FTC/substitution forward pointers → symmetry → even example → accumulation application → optional convergence sketch → recap teasing Topic 25.
teaching_arc.destination: Students define ∫ as a limit, interpret net signed area, use properties and symmetry, and set up accumulation integrals, ready for FTC in Topic 25.

topic_goal: Define the definite integral as the limit of Riemann sums; interpret net signed area and accumulated change; apply basic properties and symmetry, defer FTC and substitution to Topics 25–26.
topic_takeaways: State the definite integral as the limit of Riemann sums
Interpret ∫ as net signed area (not always total geometric area)
Apply additivity, linearity, comparison, and reversal properties
Use even/odd symmetry on symmetric intervals
Set up accumulation problems as integrals of a rate function
style_notes: Welcome back, do NOT re-introduce Arian from scratch.
Callback Topic 23: left/right/midpoint sums, Σ notation, slice-and-sum language.
Roadmap currentId path_topic_24; Topic 25 FTC next; Topic 26 substitution upcoming.
Do NOT fully teach FTC or u-substitution, s08/s09 are brief forward pointers only.
riemann_integral widget is the main visual on teaching slides; narrate n and method changes.
Use math_solution_steps for Riemann limit (s03), rectangle geometry (s07), symmetry (s11), accumulation setup (s12).
s14 summary must tease Topic 25 FTC by name.
transition_style: Move from a visual question to the formal calculus language, then close with a tiny test students can try immediately.

slides.narration_seed: Welcome back to Arian University. Topic 23 built Riemann sums, today we take the limit and define the definite integral.
slides.transition_from_previous: Open from Topic 23 Riemann sums: today the finite sum becomes a limit.
slides.transition_to_next: Carry into Learning Objectives with roadmap at path_topic_24.
slides.explanation_style: Frame definite integral as the exact limit of Topic 23 approximations.
slides.delivery_goal: Students know this topic formalizes Riemann sums into ∫.

slides.narration_seed: Roadmap: Topic 23 done, Topic 24 current, Topic 25 FTC next. Objectives focus on definition, net area, properties, symmetry, accumulation, not FTC evaluation yet.
must_cover: Define the definite integral as the limit of Riemann sums
Interpret net signed area and accumulated change
slides.transition_from_previous: Bridge from title into scoped objectives.
slides.transition_to_next: Move into rate → net change motivation.
slides.explanation_style: Orient on course_path before listing objectives.
slides.story_hint: Name Topic 23 callback and Topic 25 teaser.
slides.delivery_goal: Students know the route and what is explicitly deferred.

slide_ref.source_title: From Changing Rates to Net Change

slides.narration_seed: Callback Topic 23: slice the rate curve, sum rectangles. Limit of that sum is net change, the definite integral.
must_cover: Varying rate → total change via summing small contributions
Area under rate curve = accumulated quantity
slides.transition_from_previous: Shift from objectives into visible accumulation.
likely_student_confusion: Use prompt: real-world non-constant rate example.
slides.delivery_goal: Students connect rate graphs to accumulated change.

slides.narration_seed: ∫ is sum. Display limit of Σ f(x_i*)Δx. Widget: increase n on [1,3] with right endpoints.
must_cover: ∫_a^b f(x) dx = lim Σ f(x_i*) Δx
Δx = (b-a)/n; sample point in each subinterval
slides.transition_to_next: Pause warm-up: work the limit for 2x+1 on [1,3].
slides.delivery_goal: Students can write the limit definition.

slides.narration_seed: math_solution_steps: Δx, right endpoints, split sums, limit → 10. step_1..step_6 reveal order. Pause before step_6.
slides.transition_from_previous: Apply definition to a concrete integrand.
likely_student_confusion: Pause: try n=4 right endpoints before limit.
slides.delivery_goal: Students can execute a Riemann-sum limit computation.

slides.narration_seed: visual_lab: drive n from 4 to 30. Name sums 9, 9.6, 9.9+ approaching 10. Try left/mid/right, same limit.
slides.transition_from_previous: From algebra to full-width visual lab.
slides.transition_to_next: Interpret what happens when f crosses the axis.
slides.explanation_style: Let the lab carry the limit intuition.

slides.narration_seed: Signed strips: above +, below −. Net ∫ vs total ∫|f|. misconception_compare. Not FTC, id s05 preserved.
must_cover: Net signed area vs total geometric area
slides.transition_from_previous: Definition gives a number, now interpret its sign.
slides.transition_to_next: Properties that follow from the sum definition.
likely_student_confusion: Below-axis strips subtract from the integral.
slides.explanation_style: Contrast net and total area explicitly.
slides.delivery_goal: Students distinguish net area from total area.

slides.narration_seed: math_table: additivity, linearity, comparison, reversal, zero width. Widget timeline on subintervals.
slides.story_hint: No antiderivative needed for these rules.
slides.delivery_goal: Students can cite and use basic integral properties.

slides.narration_seed: math_solution_steps ∫_0^3 4 dx: constant → rectangle → 12. No FTC. Pause before answer.
slides.transition_from_previous: Apply definition/properties to a simple geometry case.
slides.transition_to_next: Tease FTC as next topic's shortcut.
slides.delivery_goal: Students evaluate a constant integrand geometrically.

slide_ref.source_title: Coming Next: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus

slides.narration_seed: Forward pointer only: F(b)−F(a) preview in theorem_box. Topic 25 next. Do not teach FTC here.
slides.transition_from_previous: Geometry works for simple cases, need a general shortcut.
slides.explanation_style: Teaser slide, not a full lesson.
slides.story_hint: Many rectangles vs one antiderivative evaluation.
slides.delivery_goal: Students know FTC is next and why it matters.

slides.narration_seed: Topic 26 preview: u, du, transform limits. No worked example. Name variable-mismatch trap.
slides.transition_from_previous: After FTC teaser, flag substitution bookkeeping.
likely_student_confusion: Mixing x-limits in u-integrals, fixed in Topic 26.
slides.explanation_style: Checklist only, no full u-sub computation.
slides.delivery_goal: Students know substitution is deferred and what to watch for.

slides.narration_seed: Even → double; odd → zero. sin odd on [−π,π]; cos even. formula_block rules.
slides.transition_from_previous: Symmetry as a computation shortcut within Topic 24.
likely_student_confusion: Prompt: sin vs cos parity on [−π,π].

slides.narration_seed: math_solution_steps ∫_{-2}^2 x⁴: even check, double half, 32/5 per half, 64/5 total. Odd x⁵ prompt → 0.
Setup ∫_0^4 (10+2 sin t) dt as volume. Riemann estimate ≈43.3 gal. Initial 50 → ≈93.3 gal. Exact eval in Topic 25.
slides.story_hint: Flow rate r(t) gallons per hour.
slides.delivery_goal: Students set up accumulation integrals from rates.

slides.narration_seed: Optional proof_sketch: thinner strips → smaller error → sum stabilizes. Skippable. FTC proof in Topic 25.
Recap: definition, net area, properties, symmetry, accumulation. Tease Topic 25 FTC and Topic 26 substitution. Self-check questions.
must_say: Next up, The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.
slides.explanation_style: One beat per bullet; explicit Topic 25 teaser.
slides.delivery_goal: Students leave with Topic 24 compressed and ready for FTC.
